page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n4.large is an entry-level virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 2 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 8163 cores running at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the ecs.n4.large delivers entry-level performance. The shared CPU allocation model limits compute throughput, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core score of 1107.98 and a multi-core score of 2199.5. Memory performance is similarly constrained, with uncached read bandwidth at 17.08 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 14.55 GB/sec. Real-world simulation benchmarks reflect these limitations, showing a static web serving throughput of 1.82 GB/sec and a Redis SET performance of 3.0 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance is positioned as a budget-friendly option for workloads that do not require sustained high-performance CPU or memory resources. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation, which can introduce performance variability depending on co-tenant activity. Additionally, the lack of bundled local storage and baseline network bandwidth means storage and networking requirements must be met via external attachments, which may impact overall latency and throughput.

The ecs.n4.large is suitable for low-intensity, non-critical workloads. Appropriate use cases include lightweight web serving, basic text processing, development and testing environments, and simple background tasks that can tolerate variable CPU performance and do not require high memory or storage bandwidth.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n4.large is an entry-level, KVM-based virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance utilizes a shared CPU allocation model and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU resources. Benchmark data indicates modest performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1107.98 and a multi-core score of 2199.5, alongside a static web serving throughput of 1.82 GB/sec. The instance is a budget-friendly option for low-intensity workloads, but introduces tradeoffs in CPU consistency and network throughput. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, development environments, and basic background tasks.

tagline: A budget-friendly, shared-core virtual machine designed for lightweight utility workloads, development environments, and basic web serving tasks.

Equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
